zh.lifeSummary: 當Anna de la Haye於1010年2月9日出生在法國布列塔尼易爾-吉維拉傑盧可時，她的父親Ranulf de la Haye50歲，母親Geva or Eva Duceline Crepon41歲。她和Sir Thurston Le Boteler至少有1個兒子。她去世於1095年在法國曼其傑爾布格享年85歲，埋葬在法國曼其傑爾布格。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Anna de la Haye于1010年2月9日出生在法国布列塔尼易爾-吉維拉傑盧可时，她的父亲Ranulf de la Haye50岁，母亲Geva or Eva Duceline Crepon41岁。她和Sir Thurston Le Boteler至少有1个儿子。她去世于1095年在法国曼其傑爾布格享年85岁，埋葬在法国曼其傑爾布格。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Haye
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Helmut, Willi.

North German, East Frisian, and Dutch: from a personal name, Frisian Hajo, ancient Germanic Haio; or from a short form of the female name Hadewig, composed of the elements hadu ‘battle, strife’ + wīg ‘war’.
German: variant of   Hey   4, 5.

variants.name: Deshayes
meanings: French: topographic name for someone who lived by an enclosure, from the plural of Old French haye ‘hedge’ (see Haye and Hayes ), with fused preposition and definite article des ‘from the’; or a habitational name denoting someone from Les Hayes, the name of several places in various parts of France. Compare Deshaies .

variants.name: Hayenga
meanings: East Frisian: patronymic from the personal name Hajo (see Haye 1). It is formed with the suffix -enga, a variant of -inga (from the patronymic suffix -ing and genitive plural ending -a).

variants.name: Hey
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): variant of   Hay  .
Dutch: variant, mostly Americanized (or archaic) and Flemish, of Heij, a topographic name for someone who lived on a heath, Dutch hei, heide.
German: metonymic occupational name for a grower or mower of grass, from Middle High German höu ‘grass, hay’.

variants.name: Hayen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alphonse.  German Heinz, Wilhelm.
Flemish, East Frisian, and North German: patronymic from   Haye   1.

nameMeanings.name: Anna
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: variant of Anne , in common use as a given name in most European languages. Among people with a classical education, it has from time to time been associated with Virgil's Aeneid, where it is borne by the sister of Dido , Queen of Carthage. This Phoenician name may ultimately be of Semitic origin, and thus related to the biblical Anne. However, the connection, if it exists, is indirect rather than direct.

variants.name: Anne
meanings: Pet form of   Ann   or   Anne .

en.lifeSummary: When Anna de la Haye was born on 9 February 1010, in Cherrueix, Ille-et-Vilaine, Brittany, France, her father, Ranulf de la Haye, was 50 and her mother, Geva or Eva Duceline Crepon, was 41. She had at least 1 son with Sir Thurston Le Boteler. She died in 1095, in Cherbourg, Manche, France, at the age of 85, and was buried in Cherbourg, Manche, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Anna de la Haye est née le 9 février 1010, à Cherrueix, Ille-et-Vilaine, Bretagne, France, son père, Ranulf de la Haye, avait 50 ans et sa mère, Geva or Eva Duceline Crepon, avait 41 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec Sir Thurston Le Boteler. Elle est décédée en 1095, à Cherbourg, Manche, France, à l’âge de 85 ans, et a été enterrée à Cherbourg, Manche, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Anna de la Haye am 9. Februar 1010 in Cherrueix, Ille-et-Vilaine, Bretagne, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Ranulf de la Haye 50 und ihre Mutter Geva or Eva Duceline Crepon 41. Sie hatte mit Sir Thurston Le Boteler mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1095 im Alter von 85 Jahren in Cherbourg, Manche, Frankreich und wurde in Cherbourg, Manche, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Anna de la Haye è nata il il 9 febbraio 1010 nel Cherrueix, Ille-et-Vilaine, Bretagne, Francia, sua padre, Ranulf de la Haye, aveva 50 anni e sua madre, Geva or Eva Duceline Crepon, aveva 41 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con Sir Thurston Le Boteler. Morì nel 1095, nel Cherbourg, Manche, Francia, all'età di 85 anni, e fu sepolta a Cherbourg, Manche, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Anna de la Hayeが1010年2月9日にフランスブレタグンイレ-エト-ヴィレアインチェルエイックスで生まれた時，父Ranulf de la Hayeは50歳，母Geva or Eva Duceline Creponは41歳であった。彼女はSir Thurston Le Botelerとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼女は1095年にフランスマンチェチェルボウルグで85歳で死去し，フランスマンチェチェルボウルグにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Anna de la Haye(이)가 1010년 2월 9일 프랑스 브레타그니 이레-트-빌레이인 처루이스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Ranulf de la Haye(은)는 50세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Geva or Eva Duceline Crepon(은)는 41세였다. 그녀는 Sir Thurston Le Boteler(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1095년 프랑스 먼체 처보우르그에서 85세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 먼체 처보우르그에 묻혔다.

mn.Arrow, Warwickshire, England, United Kin: Arrow, Wæringscīr, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Anna de la Haye nasceu em 9 de fevereiro de 1010, em Cherrueix, Ille-et-Vilaine, Bretagne, França, seu pai, Ranulf de la Haye, tinha 50 anos e sua mãe, Geva or Eva Duceline Crepon, tinha 41 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho com Sir Thurston Le Boteler. Ela faleceu em 1095, em Cherbourg, Manche, França, com 85 anos, e foi sepultada em Cherbourg, Manche, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Anna de la Haye родилась 9 февраля 1010 г. в местности чирруикс, илле-ет-вилеин, бретагн, Франция, её отцу по имени Ranulf de la Haye было 50 лет, а матери, которую звали Geva or Eva Duceline Crepon, 41. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от Sir Thurston Le Boteler. Она умерла в 1095 г. в местности чирбург, манч, Франция в возрасте 85 лет и была похоронена в местности чирбург, манч, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Anna de la Haye nació el 9 de febrero de 1010, en Cherrueix, Ille-et-Vilaine, Bretagne, Francia, su papá, Ranulf de la Haye, tenía 50 años y su mamá, Geva or Eva Duceline Crepon, tenía 41 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Sir Thurston Le Boteler. Murió en 1095, en Cherbourg, Manche, Francia, a la edad de 85 años, y fue sepultada en Cherbourg, Manche, Francia.
